growth_records.strain: Not applicable (undefined multispecies bioreactor community; four dominant guilds by metagenomic abundance)
growth_records.medium: Continuous bioreactor fed lignocellulosic biorefinery conversion residue (complex organic substrate)
growth_records.cite: Scarborough et al. Diagnosing and Predicting Mixed-Culture Fermentations with Unicellular and Guild-Based Metabolic Models. mSystems (2020). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00755-20. [PMC7527139]
growth_records.snippet: We constrained iFermCell215 by setting the growth rate of the model to 0.0069 h−1, corresponding to the bioreactor retention time of 6 days...The bioreactor performance data are from day 96 of reactor operations describe
growth_records.notes: mu = 1/retention time (chemostat steady state). Paper explicitly states retention time = 6 days and states this corresponds to growth rate 0.0069 h-1 (we verified: 1/(6 d x 24 h/d) = 0.00694 h-1, matches paper's reported value). Reactor is a real self-assembled mixed microbial community (not a single named prokaryote species) fed lignocellulosic biorefinery residue for 96 days; data originate from a previously published bioreactor (Scarborough et al. 2018) and are used here as a constraint for a guild-based genome-scale model. Temperature (35 C) and neutral pH are generic model assumptions stated for the thermodynamic calculations in Methods, not confirmed as the exact bioreactor operating temperature/pH; flagged medium confidence for that reason. A second growth rate (0.0051 h-1, corresponding to a model-predicted 8-day retention time) appears in the same paragraph as a MODEL PREDICTION (not an independent experimental observation) and was not recorded as a separate entry.

growth_records.strain: Mineral-medium SBR enrichment; inoculum = anaerobic digester sludge (Harnaschpolder WWTP, NL)

up.method: Calculated from mu_max and Yx,s via Herbert-Pirt (maintenance neglected); mu_max observed from base dosage, Yx,s measured (n=3)
Derived: 1.17 C-mol_S/C-mol_X/h x (1 mol glucose/6 C-mol_S) x (1 C-mol_X/24.6 gDW) = 1.17/147.6 = 0.00793 mol/gDW/h = 7.93 mmol/gDW/h; biomass CH1.8O0.5N0.2 = 24.6 g/C-mol

quantified as Cmol/Cmol sugar (Figure 1; numeric value in figure only)
sec.method: HPLC; dominant product (acetate-butyrate fermentation, ~42% glucose via acetate-butyrate pathway w/ electron bifurcation)

growth_records.cite: Rombouts, Julius Laurens et al. Selecting for lactic acid producing and utilising bacteria in anaerobic enrichment cultures. Biotechnology and Bioengineering (2020). DOI:10.1002/bit.27301. [PMC7187302]
growth_records.method: reported (mu_max = 0.17 +/- 0.02 h-1, observed from online base-dosage data after 20 SRTs)
growth_records.snippet: Enrichment medium Mineral: mu^max 0.17 +/- 0.02 hr-1; qs^max 1.17 +/- 0.30 C-mol S C-mol X-1 hr-1; Yx,s 0.15 +/- 0.04; YATP,s 3.04; Yx,ATP 7.0. Hydrogen was the major gaseous product in the mineral medium enrichment (13% +/- 1% of incoming
growth_records.notes: Anaerobic SBR mixed enrichment on 4 g/L glucose mineral medium, T=30C, pH=5.0, HRT=SRT=12h (D=1/12=0.0833 h-1). mu_max=0.17 h-1 measured from online base-dosage (Table 1). Doubling time td=ln2/mu=0.693/0.17=4.08 h. q_s^max=1.17 C-mol_S/C-mol_X/h reported (calc via Herbert-Pirt from mu and Yx,s, maintenance neglected). mmol/gDW/h conversion: glucose is C6 so 1 C-mol_S=1/6 mol glucose; biomass formula CH1.8O0.5N0.2 => M=12.011+1.8144+7.9995+2.8014=24.63 g/C-mol; q_s=1.17/(6*24.6)=7.93 mmol glucose/gDW/h. Community dominated by Ethanoligenens (Ruminococcaceae) + minor Clostridium cluster XII; acetate-butyrate-H2 fermentation. Per-product secretion rates/fractions are in Figure 1 (image, exact numbers not in machine-readable text) so left null; only H2=13% COD given in text. Model flux shares from Table S5.

sec.units: C-mol ethanol / C-mol sugar (product yield, at 31 SRTs)

Cmol/Cmol sugar (Figure 1; from secondary lactate fermentation)

sec.method: Online NGA 2000 gas analyser; only minor amount

mfa.note: Best-fit flux model, Table S5; lactate subsequently fermented to propionate/butyrate/valerate/H2/CO2

growth_records.cite: Rombouts, Julius Laurens et al. Selecting for lactic acid producing and utilising bacteria in anaerobic enrichment cultures. Biotechnology and Bioengineering (2020). DOI:10.1002/bit.27301. [PMC7187302]
growth_records.method: reported (mu_max = 0.27 +/- 0.01 h-1, observed from online base-dosage data after 20 SRTs)
growth_records.snippet: Complex: mu^max 0.27 +/- 0.01 hr-1; qs^max 2.27 +/- 0.11 C-mol S C-mol X-1 hr-1; Yx,s 0.12 +/- 0.00; YATP,s 2.33; Yx,ATP 7.9. After 31 SRTs, 0.19 Cmol ethanol Cmol-1 sugar was produced, and only a minor amount of hydrogen (1% +/- 0% of inco
growth_records.notes: Anaerobic SBR mixed enrichment on 4 g/L glucose + 0.8 g/L tryptone + 9 B vitamins, T=30C, pH=5.0, HRT=SRT=12h (D=0.0833 h-1). mu_max=0.27 h-1 measured (Table 1); td=ln2/mu=0.693/0.27=2.57 h. q_s^max=2.27 C-mol_S/C-mol_X/h reported. mmol/gDW/h: 2.27/(6*24.6)=15.38 mmol glucose/gDW/h (biomass CH1.8O0.5N0.2=24.6 g/C-mol). q_s^max 94% higher and mu_max 58% higher than mineral enrichment; biomass yield ~20-25% lower. Community: Lactococcus + Lactobacillus + Megasphaera. Two-phase: glucose->lactate+ethanol then secondary lactate fermentation to acetate/propionate/butyrate/valerate/H2/CO2 (confirmed by lactate pulse experiment). Ethanol yield 0.19 Cmol/Cmol explicit in text; other product fractions only in Figure 1 (image) so left null. Flux shares from Table S5.

growth_records.strain: Enriched Enterobacteriaceae with 100% 16S rRNA identity to Citrobacter freundii (inoculum from continuous H2-producing glycerol dark-fermentation reactor)
growth_records.medium: Minimal glycerol medium (modified from Dietz et al.), pH-buffered batch

up.units: g/L consumed (batch, over 3 days; not fully depleted at pH5 - 8.1% residual reported for initial-pH5 set)

yields.product_yields_note: molar yields per mol glycerol from Table 1 (experimental, initial pH 5)

growth_records.cite: Moscoviz, Roman et al. Consistent 1,3-propanediol production from glycerol in mixed culture fermentation over a wide range of pH. Biotechnology for Biofuels (2016). DOI:10.1186/s13068-016-0447-8. [PMC4744455]
growth_records.notes: Batch mixed-culture (open microbial consortium) glycerol dark fermentation; dominant enriched organism = Citrobacter freundii-like Enterobacteriaceae (100% 16S identity). Initial pH 5 -> final pH 4.2 +/- 0.2. Values are molar product yields per mol glycerol (Table 1), NOT specific rates (mmol/gDW/h) - no gDW-time basis reported, so qS/qP not computable. Biomass yield Y_X/S computed by authors assuming biomass C4H7O2N and Y_X/ATP=10.5 g/mol. CO2 co-produced stoichiometrically with acetate (Glycerol+2(ADP+Pi)+3NAD+ -> Acetate+CO2+...) but not separately quantified. At initial pH 5, 8.1% of initial glycerol remained after 3 days (near-complete but incomplete consumption).

up.method: slope of substrate vs time / active biomass (HPLC)

growth_records.cite: Temudo, Margarida F. et al. Xylose anaerobic conversion by open-mixed cultures. Applied Microbiology and Biotechnology (2009). DOI:10.1007/s00253-008-1749-y. [PMC7419444]
growth_records.snippet: Under substrate-limiting conditions... xylose fermentation produced more butyrate and less ethanol and the biomass yield per C mol substrate was 20% lower... Dilution rate 0.128; Biomass yield 0.16 Cmol/Cmol; -qs 0.89 Cmol/Cmol X h.
growth_records.notes: Continuous chemostat, xylose 0.133 Cmol/L, pH8, 30C, D=0.128/h. mu=D=0.128/h; td=ln2/0.128=5.42 h. Reported specific uptake -qs=0.89 Cmol/Cmol X/h (Table 2). Secretion rates DERIVED = product-yield(Table1) x qs(0.89): acetate 0.18x0.89=0.160; butyrate 0.46x0.89=0.409; ethanol 0.017x0.89=0.0151; formate 0.20x0.89=0.178; H2 0.13x0.89=0.116. Product yields are experimental (Table 1). Yields relative to Cmol substrate.

growth_records.notes: Glucose pulse 0.083 Cmol/L into xylose chemostat; -qs=0.78, Yxs=0.143 (Table 2). Higher biomass yield than xylose confirms glucose more efficient. 30C pH8 anaerobic. Glucose conversion capacity constitutively present in xylose community.

growth_records.snippet: During the pulse with the two substrates, both substrates were simultaneously consumed in the beginning (first 2 h)... The glucose uptake rate was approximately 20% lower than the xylose uptake rate. Table 2: Xylose+glucose 0.167 combined -
growth_records.notes: Simultaneous xylose+glucose pulse (each 0.083 Cmol/L). No substrate preference; both consumed together. -qs xylose 0.56, glucose 0.41 (glucose ~20% lower), combined 0.97; combined Yxs=0.111 (Table 2, Phase 1 = first 3 h). 30C pH8 anaerobic.

yield_fit.note: qS = (1/Yxs)·µ + m_s  — linear fit of carbon-source uptake vs growth rate
